items.title: Sudan: Rights Group Reports Fresh Arrests of Sudanese Miners By Egyptian Forces
items.description: [Dabanga] Waidi El Atri / El Rataj / Argeen / Cairo -- A Sudanese human rights organisation has accused Egyptian forces of carrying out a fresh operation against Sudanese gold miners near the Sudan-Egypt border, arresting dozens of people days after a deadly incident that survivors say killed more than 50 miners inside Sudanese territory.
items.titleIt: Sudan: Rights Group Reports Fresh Arrests of Sudanese Miners By Egyptian Forces

items.title: Somalia: EU Imposes New Visa Restrictions On Somali Citizens
items.description: [Shabelle] Brussels, June 25, 2026 -- The European Union has temporarily tightened visa rules for Somali citizens, citing insufficient cooperation from Somalia in the readmission of its nationals who are staying illegally in EU member states.
items.titleIt: Somalia: EU Imposes New Visa Restrictions On Somali Citizens

items.title: Pope made us feel that writers are useful says Igiaba Scego
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - Pope Leo XIV made an international delegation of authors feel that writers are useful during an audience Wednesday marking the 100th anniversary of the founding of Vatican publisher Libreria Editrice Vataicnaa (LEV), Italo-Somali novelist Igiaba Scego told ANSA after their encounter with the American pontiff. "Pope Leo made us feel that writers are very useful. Fiction is often marginalized, and yet, precisely because we live in a world of artificial intelligence and unbridled capitalism, understanding that storytelling is the center of life was important. The Pope's wo
items.titleIt: Pope made us feel that writers are useful says Igiaba Scego

items.title: Libya's eastern government bans entry for nationals of four countries
items.description: The decree prohibits citizens of Sudan, Eritrea, Ethiopia, and Somalia from entering through all land, sea, and air ports.
items.titleIt: Libya's eastern government bans entry for nationals of four countries

items.title: PM's office asks 2 mn euro Regeni compensation
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - Premier Giorgia Meloni's office on Wednesday requested compensation of two million euros from four Egyptian national security officials charged with abducting and torturing to death Italian student Giulio Regeni in Cairo in January-February 2016. Speaking at their in-absentia trial in Rome, the State Attorney said that "this crime has profoundly affected the Italian community" and that the defendants "have violated the rights to the protection of Italian citizens' freedoms, even abroad. "Constitutionally guaranteed rights have been violated." The 28-year-old student's h
items.titleIt: PM's office asks 2 mn euro Regeni compensation

items.title: Kenya: Amnesty Condemns Uganda Over Karua Detention and Deportation
items.description: [Capital FM] Nairobi -- Amnesty International has condemned the detention and deportation of Kenyan human rights lawyer Martha Karua by Ugandan authorities, describing the incident as "unlawful and outrageous."
items.titleIt: Kenya: Amnesty Condemns Uganda Over Karua Detention and Deportation

items.title: Nairobi signs $1.2 billion airport expansion deal with China Road and Bridge Corp
items.description: Kenya has signed a $1.2 billion deal with China Road and Bridge Corporation to expand Nairobi’s Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, the country’s transport minister said on Tuesday.
items.titleIt: Nairobi signs $1.2 billion airport expansion deal with China Road and Bridge Corp

items.title: Mexico Celebrates the Third “Made in Italy” Day
items.description: (ANSA) - CITTÀ DEL MESSICO, 25 GIU - The third edition of the National “Made in Italy” Day, titled “Italian Technological Excellence in Mexico in Strategic Sectors,” was held in the Mexican capital. The event was dedicated to highlighting the technological and industrial contributions of Italian companies in Mexico. The Italian Ambassador, Alessandro Modiano, confirmed the strength of trade ties, noting that “the main sector for Italian exports to Mexico is industrial machinery, which accounts for nearly 40% of the total.” Director Gravela highlighted the ICE Agency’s commitment to promoting I
items.titleIt: Mexico Celebrates the Third “Made in Italy” Day

items.title: EU countries give 1st green light to aid for soaring fertiliser prices
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 25 - EU member states have given rapid approval to support measures aimed at helping farmers cope with rising fertiliser costs driven by the conflict in the Middle East. The proposal for targeted amendments to the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP), presented last week by the European Commission, will now enter negotiations with the European Parliament. Under the current 2021-2027 CAP framework, Brussels has proposed a temporary liquidity scheme, the possibility for member states to advance direct payments at a higher rate in 2026, and greater flexibility to reprogramme funds
items.titleIt: EU countries give 1st green light to aid for soaring fertiliser prices

items.title: European Parliament activates urgent procedure for fertiliser price support measures
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 25 - The European Parliament has activated the urgent procedure to approve support measures for farmers affected by soaring fertiliser prices linked to the conflict in the Middle East. The proposal for targeted amendments to the Common Agricultural Policy was presented by the European Commission last week to provide emergency support to the sector. The measures will be put to a vote during Parliament's July plenary session. Within the current 2021-2027 CAP framework, Brussels has proposed establishing a temporary liquidity scheme, allowing member states to advance direct pay
items.titleIt: European Parliament activates urgent procedure for fertiliser price support measures

items.title: EU announces up to €540 million in aid for farmers and greater CAP flexibility
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 25 - The European Commission has announced that up to €540 million from the CAP agricultural reserve could be mobilised to provide "targeted and exceptional" support to farmers affected by the conflict in the Middle East. EU member states are expected to approve the increase in available resources by the end of July. Brussels has also proposed a series of targeted CAP amendments, including a temporary liquidity scheme, greater flexibility to advance direct payments and the possibility to reprogramme funds to help farmers cope with rising fertiliser prices. Earlier this week,
items.titleIt: EU announces up to €540 million in aid for farmers and greater CAP flexibility

items.title: Sexual violence used as a 'weapon of war' since start of Sudan conflict, UN says
items.description: Sexual violence has being used “consistently as a tactic and weapon of war” since the conflict in Sudan erupted three years ago. That’s the finding of a new report by the United Nations Human Rights Office.
items.titleIt: Sexual violence used as a 'weapon of war' since start of Sudan conflict, UN says

items.title: Rutte's statements on US flights from Italy during Iran war misleading - defence min
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - The defence ministry said Wednesday that NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's assertion that 500 US flights took off from American bases in Italy during Operation Epic Fury was misleading, stressing that Rome had only authorized the bases to be used for ordinary operations during the Iran war and not for offensive combat missions. Rutte's comments sparked a political row here, with opposition politicians saying they conflict with the government's statements about the use of the bases and calling on Premier Giorgia Meloni to report to parliament to clarify. During the war
items.titleIt: Rutte's statements on US flights from Italy during Iran war misleading - defence min

items.title: Ambassador Fontana Meets with Iraqi Minister of Industry
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 24 GIU - As part of the ongoing economic dialogue between Italy and Iraq, the Italian Ambassador to Baghdad, Niccolò Fontana, met with the Iraqi Minister of Industry and Mineral Resources, Mohammed Nouri Ahmed. The meeting provided an opportunity to explore prospects for strengthening bilateral industrial cooperation and to examine opportunities for collaboration and investment in strategic sectors such as the chemical industry, silicates, cement, silica sand, and the pharmaceutical sector. The meeting reaffirmed the shared commitment to further developing economic and industria
items.titleIt: Ambassador Fontana Meets with Iraqi Minister of Industry

items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUN 24 - The defence ministry said Wednesday that NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's assertion that 500 US flights took off from American bases in Italy during Operation Epic Fury was misleading, stressing that Rome had only authorized the bases to be used for ordinary operations during the Iran war and not for offensive combat missions. Rutte's comments sparked a political row here, with opposition politicians saying they conflict with the government's statements about the use of the bases and calling on Premier Giorgia Meloni to report to parliament to clarify. items.titleIt: Rutte's statements on US flights from Italy misleading says defence ministry

items.title: Morocco: Morocco's Hidden History - Archaeology, DNA and Carbon Dating Rewrite the Story of the Ancient World
items.description: [The Conversation Africa] For decades, stories about the ancient Mediterranean have centred on the grand cultures of Greece, Rome, Phoenicia and Egypt. North-west Africa seldom enters the picture before the arrival of Phoenician traders on the Moroccan coast about 3,000 years ago.
items.titleIt: Morocco: Morocco's Hidden History - Archaeology, DNA and Carbon Dating Rewrite the Story of the Ancient World
